articles.title: Body of Indian sailor missing after vessel attack near Oman found
articles.summary: Body of Indian sailor missing after vessel attack near Oman found The body of an Indian sailor who went missing after his ship was attacked off the coast of Oman has been recovered, a seafarers' union official has said. Heramb Karmarkar, a 30-year-old marine engineer from the western Indian city of Pune, had been missing since Sunday's attack on the Cypriot-flagged GFS Galaxy. "I got a call Tuesday evening from the company the ship belonged to informing me that Heramb Karmarkar's body has been found by the Omani coast guard," Manoj Yadav, of the Forward Seamen's Union of India, told AFP.&

Israeli air strike on home kills four members of the same family
articles.summary: Israeli air strike on home kills four members of the same family The death toll from an Israeli helicopter air strike early on Wednesday in Deir el-Balah, central Gaza Strip, has risen to four, including a six year-old child.  The attack directly struck a family home, according to the Wafa news agency, killing 33 year-old Omar Sami Ahmed Abu Qassem, his wife Asmaa Ghazi Abu Qassem and their daughter Habiba Omar Sami Abu Qassem.  The name of the fourth victim has yet to be reported.

articles.title: Iran will 'never' request talks with US, deputy foreign minister says
articles.summary: Iran will 'never' request talks with US, deputy foreign minister says Iran's  Deputy Foreign Minister stressed that Iran  "will never request negotiations with the United States.," state TV IRIB quoted him as saying.  "Talking about international law during wartime is a #joke," Kazem Gharibabadi said. "U.S. can’t come teach us about international law!" He added that while Oman shared the Strait of Hormuz, it is "wartime now, and for national security reasons Iran must control all of the Strait".

articles.title: IRGC warns energy exports would be 'for everyone or for no one' if US interferes in maritime routes
articles.summary: IRGC warns energy exports would be 'for everyone or for no one' if US interferes in maritime routes The IRGC warned that if Washington sought to block the region's energy exports by controlling maritime routes, other routes serving US and allied interests could also be shut down. It added that regional energy exports would be "for everyone or for no one".

articles.title: Iran claims attacks across the region, including in Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait
articles.summary: Iran claims attacks across the region, including in Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait The IRGC detailed in several statements Iran's most recent wave of attacks across the region, with targets in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan.  Drones were used to target the al-Azraq base in Jordan, state TV IRIB reported. Meanwhile, cruise missiles were fired at a US military logistics centre at Mina Abdullah in Kuwait. Facilities used by the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain were also targeted and "crushed", according to IRIB quoting the IRGC, including the "NSI Management Center, Command and Control

articles.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's power plants, bridges next week if talks not resumed
articles.summary: Trump threatens to target Iran's power plants, bridges next week if talks not resumed US President Donald Trump warned that Washington will begin targeting Iran's power plants and bridges next week unless Tehran returns to the negotiating table. "We're going to hit them very hard tonight. We're going to hit them very hard tomorrow night. We're going to hit them very hard the night after," Trump said during an interview with Fox News. articles.title: Bahrain sounds off sirens, Kuwait says intercepting Iranian drones
articles.summary: Bahrain sounds off sirens, Kuwait says intercepting Iranian drones Bahrain sounded an air raid siren, the interior ministry said on Wednesday, urging citizens and residents to remain calm and head to the nearest safe place. Elsewhere in Kuwait, the military said it was intercepting Iranian drones: "Kuwaiti air defenses are currently engaging hostile drone attacks following the nefarious Iranian aggression."

articles.title: Bottled water factory in western Iran struck
articles.summary: Bottled water factory in western Iran struck A bottled water factory in Iran’s western Ilam province was reportedly hit by three projectiles, according to state media.  Dehloran Governor Morad Yeganeh indicated that the strikes caused damage to equipment at the production facility , but did not identify any perpetrators. No casualties were reported.  Minutes earlier, Iran’s Fars news agency reported of fresh US attacks in the city of Dehloran, south west of Ilam.

articles.title: Head of US Central Command says Iran attacked seven commercial ships over past week
articles.summary: Head of US Central Command says Iran attacked seven commercial ships over past week The head of US Central Command said that Iran had "intentionally targeted" seven commercial ships over the past week, resulting in "nearly a dozen" civilian crew members being killed, injured or going missing. "Iranian forces have also launched dozens of missiles and drones toward neighboring Gulf countries," Admiral Brad Cooper said in a statement, adding US forces are holding Tehran accountable for "unwarranted aggression".

articles.title: Iran calls US attack on Hormozgan ‘latest war crime’
articles.summary: Iran calls US attack on Hormozgan ‘latest war crime’ Iran’s Foreign Minister spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said that the US military’s attack on a ranger post in Hormozgan is the latest example in a list of American war crimes against Iran which “grows longer every day”. Baghaei noted that the attack on Tuesday morning in the village of Seyed Jowzar in northern Hormozgan killed three members of a family working as rangers in the area. “This is merely the latest example of America’s heinous war crimes over the past four and a half months, which began with the assassination of Iranian leaders, ch

articles.title: First half of summer Spain's hottest on record: weather agency
articles.summary: Mainland Spain registered its hottest first half of the summer since records began in 1961, with two heatwaves sending temperatures soaring, national weather agency AEMET said on Tuesday. The average temperature from June 1 to July 15 was 24.5C, 3.3C higher than the figure of 21.2C for the 1991-2020 reference period, AEMET spokesman Ruben del […] The post First half of summer Spain's hottest on record: weather agency appeared first on HESPRESS English - Morocco News .
